evals.prompt: I just finished refactoring the `assistant-web` auth logic to use TanStack Router hooks. Can you log what we did and what's left to do? I modified `apps/assistant-web/src/routes/auth.tsx` and `packages/assistant-app/src/hooks/use-auth.ts`. I still need to update the mobile app.
evals.expected_output: A markdown file in .agent-docs/activity/ with UTC timestamp, proper frontmatter (type: activity), and summary of the auth refactor.
assertions: A markdown file was created inside an 'activity' subdirectory (not just in the outputs root)
The filename includes a UTC timestamp in YYYY-MM-DD_HHMMSSZ format (not hardcoded or guessed)
The frontmatter contains 'type: activity'
The frontmatter contains a 'date' field in ISO 8601 UTC format
The frontmatter contains a 'branch' field
The document body mentions both modified files (auth.tsx and use-auth.ts)
The document body mentions updating the mobile app as remaining/follow-up work

evals.prompt: We decided to switch from Lucia to Clerk for better scalability. Document the reasoning for this choice and the trade-offs involved. Mention that Clerk handles session management and social logins out of the box, while Lucia was too much manual work.
evals.expected_output: A markdown file in .agent-docs/decisions/ with UTC timestamp, proper frontmatter (type: decision), and clear options/consequences sections.
assertions: A markdown file was created inside a 'decisions' subdirectory (not just in the outputs root)
The filename includes a UTC timestamp in YYYY-MM-DD_HHMMSSZ format
The frontmatter contains 'type: decision'
The document has a section for options considered, covering both Lucia and Clerk
The document has a decision section clearly naming Clerk as the chosen option
The document has a consequences or trade-offs section
The document mentions session management or social logins as advantages of Clerk
The document mentions Lucia requiring too much manual work

evals.prompt: I've been looking into `Drizzle ORM` vs `Prisma`. Here's what I found: Drizzle is faster and has better TypeScript support, while Prisma has a nicer CLI. We should go with Drizzle. Please document this research.
evals.expected_output: A markdown file in .agent-docs/research/ with UTC timestamp, proper frontmatter (type: research), and recommendation for Drizzle.
assertions: A markdown file was created inside a 'research' subdirectory (not just in the outputs root)
The filename includes a UTC timestamp in YYYY-MM-DD_HHMMSSZ format
The frontmatter contains 'type: research'
The document has a recommendation section explicitly recommending Drizzle
The document mentions Drizzle's performance/speed advantage
The document mentions Drizzle's better TypeScript support
The document mentions Prisma's nicer CLI as an advantage of Prisma
The document has a findings or comparison section (not just a conclusion)
